drafts.text:
v1.14.1 shipped this morning.

New: pick your language, Jot selects the right on-device model automatically.

English on M2 Pro / 16 GB+ → Nemotron. French, Spanish, German and 3 more → Parakeet v3. Japanese → Parakeet JA. Other Macs → Parakeet v2.

No cloud. No API key. The right model for your language, running entirely on-device.

Free, open source, Apple Silicon.

What language do you work in?

#macOS #indiedev #buildinpublic
drafts.note: Attach lang-tip.svg (export as 1080×1080 PNG). Do NOT put the link in the post body — LinkedIn downranks posts with external links. Paste the utm_url as the FIRST COMMENT immediately after posting.

drafts.text:
Jot v1.14.1 — now picks the best on-device model for your language (free, open source Mac dictation)

Just shipped v1.14.1, which adds language-based model selection. Pick the language you speak — Jot automatically uses the best on-device model for it.

- English on M2 Pro or 16 GB+ Mac: Nemotron (NVIDIA's high-accuracy on-device model)
- English on all other Apple Silicon Macs: Parakeet v2
- French, Spanish, German, Italian, Portuguese, Dutch: Parakeet v3 multilingual
- Japanese: Parakeet JA

No downloads you didn't ask for — existing users keep their current model with no surprise changes.

The reason: until now, picking a model in Jot was a technical choice (which Parakeet variant is that again?). Language is something everyone understands. You speak French — you pick French. Done.

Jot is free, open source, Apple Silicon only, macOS 14+. No account, no subscription, no audio leaves your Mac.

(I built this — disclosure)

Link in comments.
drafts.note: Post to r/macapps (rotating: macapps Jun 11, MacOS Jun 12, productivity Jun 13, macapps Jun 14). First line is the post title, the rest is the body. Replace 'Link in comments' with the actual utm_url as the first comment. recommended:false — last recommended:true reddit post was June 11 (3 days ago); needs 4+ days — earliest is June 15. Answer every comment in the first hour.

drafts.text: Show HN: Jot 1.14.1 – free on-device Mac dictation, auto-selects the best model for your language
drafts.publish_window_pt: 6:00–8:00am ET (Tue–Thu only — hold until Tue Jun 16)
drafts.note:
v1.14.1 shipped today (Jun 14), qualifying this for Show HN. DO NOT post on weekends — hold until Tue Jun 16 at 6–8am ET. First comment backstory to paste immediately after submission:

'Jot is a free, open-source Mac dictation app I've been building since April. Press a hotkey, speak, and the transcript pastes wherever your cursor is in any app. Transcription runs entirely on the Apple Neural Engine — no internet, no account, no audio leaves the Mac.

Today's 1.14.1 adds language-based model selection. The problem: users had to choose between Parakeet v2, Parakeet v3, Nemotron in settings — meaningful if you know the model landscape, confusing if you don't. Now you just pick the language you speak. Jot maps it to the best available model: English on M2 Pro/16GB+ gets Nemotron (higher accuracy), European languages get multilingual Parakeet v3, Japanese gets Parakeet JA. Existing users keep their current model with no surprise downloads.

Apple Silicon only, macOS 14+. Source: https://github.com/vineetu/JOT-Transcribe'

Never solicit upvotes. The utm_url goes in the submission URL field.

notes: v1.14.1 shipped at 4:21am UTC today with language-aware model selection — the strongest product angle since launch. Downloads climbed to 120 (+1 from yesterday's 119; 7-day avg 8/day; new release DMG has 1 download so far, Sparkle auto-updates don't increment the GitHub counter). iTunes API returned HTTP 403 so appstore.json was not updated; June 13 entry (iOS v1.1, 1 rating at 5★) is preserved. No outreach this cycle (last: June 12, within 7-day window). No specific Reddit/HN reply threads found in today's searches. Instagram recommended:true today — threshold of 3+ days since June 11 met. The multilingual angle opens a new untouched audience: French, Spanish, German, Japanese, and other non-English speakers who've assumed Mac dictation is English-only. Consider follow-up posts in r/learnfrench, r/español, or r/LearnJapanese next week with a genuine use-case angle. Hold the Show HN until Tuesday Jun 16.
